metadata.website_type: precision cnc machining manufacturing company site

tasks.name: Submit an RFQ for a stainless steel shaft with tight tolerance and lead time under 15 business days
tasks.description: Request a quote for a CNC-turned stainless steel shaft, specifying quantity 250, a tight tolerance option, and selecting the fastest non-expedited lead time that is 15 business days or less.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
In the main navigation menu, hover over or click on the “Services” item and then select “Request a Quote” from the dropdown.
On the RFQ page, locate the machining type section and select the “CNC Turning” option.
Find the material dropdown field and select “Stainless Steel 304” as the material.
In the dimensions section, enter “120” in the length field (in millimeters) and “20” in the diameter field (in millimeters).
In the quantity field, type “250” as the required quantity.

In the tolerance dropdown, choose the tightest available option that is labeled “±0.01 mm (tight)” or, if multiple options are shown, select the one with a tolerance value less than or equal to ±0.02 mm.

Scroll to the lead time options section and open the lead time dropdown, then select the fastest option that is labeled as “Standard” (not “Expedited”) and shows a lead time of 15 business days or less.
Scroll to the contact details section and enter a name (for example, type “Alex Taylor” in the name field) and an email address (for example, type “alex.taylor@example.com” in the email field).
Click the “Submit RFQ” button at the bottom of the form.

tasks.name: Identify the tightest-tolerance 5-axis aluminum machining service and request sample project information
tasks.description: Find a 5-axis CNC machining service for aluminum capable of at least ±0.005 in tolerance, choose the service with the tightest listed tolerance for aluminum, then send a contact form message asking for a sample project using that service.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the “Capabilities” or “Services” link in the main navigation.
On the capabilities/services page, use the process filter to select “5-Axis CNC Machining.”
Use the material filter to select “Aluminum” and apply the filter.
In the tolerance filter or specification area, select or enter a maximum tolerance of “±0.005 in” and apply this filter so only services meeting this tolerance or tighter are shown.

If a sort option is available, open the sort dropdown and select an option such as “Tolerance – Tightest First” or similar; then identify the first service in the filtered list, which should have the tightest tolerance for aluminum.
Click on the title or image of this top-listed 5-axis aluminum service to open its detailed service page.
On the service detail page, locate and click the “Contact Us about this Service” button or link.

On the contact form that opens, type “Request for sample 5-axis aluminum project” in the subject field and in the message field mention that you are interested in the specific 5-axis aluminum service you just viewed, then include a brief note such as “Please share a sample project using this service.”
Fill in the name and email fields with any placeholder data (for example, name as “Jordan Lee” and email as “jordan.lee@example.com”) and click the “Send Message” or “Submit” button.

tasks.name: Compare titanium and stainless steel for a medical device part and select the cheaper option under $40 per unit
tasks.description: Use the cost estimator to compare titanium and stainless steel for a medical device component at a quantity of 100 units, each requiring fine surface finish, and choose the material whose estimated cost per unit is lower and under $40; then mark that material as the preferred choice for a quote.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
In the header navigation, click on “Resources” or “Tools” and select “Cost Estimator” from the dropdown.
On the cost estimator page, locate the part type dropdown and select a medical part type option, such as “Medical Implant / Component.”
Set the quantity field to “100” units by typing 100 into the quantity input.
In the material dropdown, select “Titanium Grade 5” and in the surface finish dropdown choose a fine finish option, such as “Ra 0.8 µm (fine).”
Click the “Calculate Cost” or similar button, and take note of the displayed estimated cost per unit for the titanium option, focusing on whether it is under $40.

Without leaving the estimator, change the material dropdown from “Titanium Grade 5” to “Stainless Steel 316L” while keeping the quantity at 100 and the same fine surface finish selected, then click “Calculate Cost” again to view the stainless steel cost per unit.
Compare the two cost-per-unit values and identify which material (Titanium Grade 5 or Stainless Steel 316L) has the lower cost per unit that is also below $40.
Use the available control (for example, a radio button or “Set as Preferred” button) next to the chosen material to mark that material as the preferred choice for the quote.
Click on the “Proceed to Quote” or “Request Quote with Preferred Material” button to continue with the selected cheaper material.

tasks.name: Find an aerospace case study with delivery under 10 days and start a similar project request
tasks.description: Locate a case study in the aerospace industry where delivery time is listed as 10 days or less, open it, and use its call-to-action to initiate a new project request referencing this case study.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the “Case Studies” or “Success Stories” link in the main navigation.
On the case studies listing page, use the industry filter and select “Aerospace” from the list, then apply the filter.
Use an additional filter or tag control (such as a lead-time or delivery time filter) to show only case studies with delivery times labeled “≤ 10 days” or “10 days or less.”

If sorting is available, open the sort dropdown and select an option such as “Fastest Delivery First,” then locate the first aerospace case study in the resulting list that shows a delivery time of 10 days or less in its summary.
Click on the title or thumbnail of that aerospace case study to open its detailed page.
On the case study detail page, scroll until you find a button or link labeled something like “Start a Similar Project,” “Request a Similar Quote,” or “Get a Similar Result,” and click it.
On the project request form that opens, enter a project name such as “Aerospace project similar to [case study title]” into the project name field.

In the project description or message field, type a short note indicating that you want a project similar to the case study you just viewed and mention that you are aiming for delivery in 10 days or less.
Fill in the name and email fields with placeholder values (for example, name as “Morgan Rivera” and email as “morgan.rivera@example.com”) and click the “Submit Request” button.

tasks.name: Build a comparison of three machines that can handle parts ≥ 500 mm with surface finish ≤ Ra 1.6 µm
tasks.description: From the equipment list, filter and select three CNC machines that support a maximum part length of at least 500 mm and can achieve a surface finish of Ra 1.6 µm or better, then add them to a comparison view.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
In the main navigation, click on “Equipment” or “Our Machines.”

On the equipment listing page, locate the part size filter and set the minimum part length to “500 mm” by entering 500 in the appropriate field or selecting it from a range control, then apply the filter.
Find the surface finish filter and set the maximum surface roughness to “Ra 1.6 µm” or select an equivalent option that indicates Ra 1.6 µm or better, then apply this filter.
If there is a sorting option, open the sort dropdown and select “Newest First” or “Most Capable First” to organize the filtered machine list.
From the filtered list, identify the first three machines displayed and for each of these three machines click the “Add to Compare” checkbox or button associated with the machine entry.
After selecting three machines, click on the “Compare (3)” or “View Comparison” button or link typically located near the top or bottom of the equipment page.
On the comparison page that opens, scroll through the comparison table to view the key specifications (such as maximum part length and surface finish capability) for the three selected machines.

If there is an option to label or save this comparison, click the “Save Comparison” or “Email Comparison to Myself” button and, if needed, enter a placeholder email like “machine.compare@example.com” to complete the action (without any authentication).
Remain on the comparison page after completing the action to finalize the comparison task.

tasks.name: Schedule a plant tour on a weekday morning next month using the visit form
tasks.description: Use the visit scheduling form to request a plant tour on a weekday morning date within the next calendar month, specifying the number of attendees as 3.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the “About Us” or “Company” link in the main navigation, then select “Visit Our Facility” or “Plant Tour” from the submenu if available.
On the plant tour or visit page, locate and click the “Schedule a Tour” or “Request a Visit” button to open the scheduling form.
In the date picker field, open the calendar and navigate to the next calendar month (relative to today) using the next-month arrow, then select any weekday date (Monday through Friday) in that month.
In the time-of-day dropdown or selector, choose a morning slot, such as “9:00–11:00 AM” or “Morning (8–12).”
In the number of attendees field, type “3” as the total number of visitors.
In the notes or comments field, type a short note such as “We would like a general tour focused on CNC machining capabilities.”
Fill in the contact name field with a placeholder name like “Taylor Morgan” and the email field with “taylor.morgan@example.com.”
If a phone number field is present, enter a placeholder number like “555-123-4567.”
Click the “Submit Tour Request” or “Send Request” button to complete the scheduling request.

tasks.name: Create a cart with 2 turning parts and 1 milling part under $150 each from the standard parts catalog
tasks.description: From the standard parts catalog, add exactly two CNC turning parts and one CNC milling part to the cart, ensuring each selected part has a unit price less than $150, then proceed to the cart review page.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the “Products” or “Standard Parts” link in the main navigation to open the catalog.
Use the category filter sidebar to select the “CNC Turning Parts” category and apply it.
Within the turning parts category, use the price filter to set the maximum price to “$150” and apply the filter so only parts priced under $150 are displayed.
From the filtered turning parts list, click on the first product card to open its detail page, set the quantity to “1” in the quantity field, and click the “Add to Cart” button.

Use the breadcrumb or navigation link to return to the turning parts category, then select the second product in the filtered list by clicking its card, set quantity to “1,” and click “Add to Cart” again.
Return to the main catalog page and change the category filter from “CNC Turning Parts” to “CNC Milling Parts.”

Apply the price filter in the milling parts category to limit items to a maximum price of “$150,” then click on the first item in the filtered milling parts list, set the quantity to “1,” and click “Add to Cart.”
Locate the cart icon or “View Cart” link in the header and click it to navigate to the cart or checkout review page.
On the cart page, scroll through the list of items and keep the cart open for review with the 3 selected parts.

tasks.name: Use the lead-time calculator to choose a shipping option delivering 500 anodized parts within 12 business days
tasks.description: Use the online lead-time calculator to estimate production and shipping for 500 machined parts requiring anodizing and inspection, then select a shipping method whose total lead time is 12 business days or less and proceed to the summary page.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
In the main navigation, click on “Resources” or “Tools” and select “Lead Time Calculator” from the dropdown.
On the lead-time calculator page, type “500” into the quantity field for number of parts.
In the additional processes or finishes section, check the box for “Anodizing” and also check the box for “Dimensional Inspection” if available.
Click the “Calculate Lead Time” or similar button to generate available production and shipping options.
In the list of calculated options, locate the shipping or service level dropdown and open it to view options such as “Standard,” “Expedited,” and “Express.”

Review the displayed total business days for each option and click to select the option whose total lead time (production plus shipping) is 12 business days or less; if multiple options qualify, select the one with the lowest cost shown.
After selecting the qualifying option, click the “Continue” or “View Summary” button to proceed to the lead-time summary page.
On the summary page, scroll through the details that show the chosen quantity, anodizing and inspection processes, and the selected shipping method.
Remain on the summary page to complete the lead-time planning task.

entities.name: Service
entities.description: Machining service/capability offering (e.g., 5-axis CNC machining for aluminum). Used on Capabilities, Service Detail, and to relate to inquiries and case studies.

fields.name: slug
fields.description: URL-safe identifier

fields.name: supported_industries
fields.description: Array of industry enum values

fields.name: industry_values_definition
fields.description: Reference list for values used inside supported_industries array

fields.name: min_tolerance_mm
fields.description: Best overall achievable tolerance for this service in mm (regardless of material)

fields.name: min_tolerance_in
fields.description: Best overall achievable tolerance for this service in inches

fields.name: best_surface_finish_ra_um
fields.description: Best achievable surface roughness Ra in µm

fields.name: example_applications
fields.description: Array of strings describing typical applications

fields.name: related_case_study_ids
fields.description: Array of CaseStudy ids shown as related examples

entities.name: ServiceMaterialSpec
entities.description: Per-material capability details for a service, used for filtering by material and tolerance on the Capabilities page.

fields.name: min_tolerance_mm
fields.description: Best achievable tolerance for this material on this service in mm

fields.name: min_tolerance_in
fields.description: Best achievable tolerance for this material on this service in inches

fields.name: best_surface_finish_ra_um
fields.description: Best surface finish (Ra µm) this service can achieve on this material

entities.name: ServiceInquiry
entities.description: Inquiry submitted via 'Contact Us about this Service' on a service detail page.

entities.name: RequestQuote
entities.description: RFQ submissions for custom machined parts from the Request a Quote page or other tools.

fields.name: tolerance_label
fields.description: Display label such as '±0.01 mm (tight)'

fields.name: tolerance_value_mm
fields.description: Numeric ± tolerance in mm

fields.name: tolerance_value_in
fields.description: Numeric ± tolerance in inches

fields.name: lead_time_business_days
fields.description: Selected quoted lead time in business days

fields.name: drawing_attachment_urls
fields.description: Array of URLs to uploaded drawings/CAD files

fields.name: special_requirements
fields.description: Notes on finishes, inspections, etc.

fields.name: preferred_cost_estimate_id
fields.description: Id of CostEstimate used to prefill this RFQ

entities.name: CostEstimate
entities.description: Cost estimator configurations and results used to compare materials and pass a preferred configuration into an RFQ.

fields.name: is_preferred
fields.description: True when user has marked this configuration as preferred for quoting

entities.name: CaseStudy
entities.description: Customer case studies and success stories, filterable by industry and delivery time.

fields.name: processes_used
fields.description: Array of Service.process_type enum values

fields.name: materials
fields.description: Array of material enum values

fields.name: delivery_time_days
fields.description: Actual project delivery time in days

entities.name: SimilarProjectRequest
entities.description: Form submissions created from a case study via 'Start a Similar Project'.

fields.name: target_delivery_days
fields.description: Desired delivery timeframe mentioned by user

entities.name: Machine
entities.description: CNC machines and equipment with specs, used on Equipment listing and comparison pages.

fields.name: max_part_length_mm
fields.description: Maximum supported part length

fields.name: min_surface_roughness_ra_um
fields.description: Best achievable Ra µm; smaller is better

fields.name: min_tolerance_mm
fields.description: Best achievable tolerance in mm

fields.name: min_tolerance_in
fields.description: Best achievable tolerance in inches

entities.name: EquipmentComparison
entities.description: Saved comparison of selected machines, including email/share actions.

fields.name: machine_ids
fields.description: Array of Machine ids included in this comparison

fields.name: emailed_to
fields.description: Email address the comparison was sent to, if any

entities.name: Product
entities.description: Standard catalog parts (turning and milling) that can be added to the cart.

entities.name: Cart
entities.description: Shopping cart for standard parts. Single active cart is sufficient for this single-user scenario.

entities.name: CartItem
entities.description: Line items within the Cart referencing catalog products.

fields.name: line_total
fields.description: quantity * unit_price

entities.name: PlantTourRequest
entities.description: Requests submitted via the Visit Our Facility / Schedule a Tour form.

fields.name: visit_date
fields.description: Date selected in date picker (time portion can be normalized)

entities.name: LeadTimeCalculation
entities.description: Lead-time calculator runs capturing quantity and selected processes.

fields.name: processes
fields.description: Array of additional process enum values

fields.name: process_values_definition
fields.description: Reference list for values used inside processes array

entities.name: LeadTimeOption
entities.description: Production + shipping options calculated for a given lead-time calculation (e.g., Standard, Expedited, Express).

fields.name: total_lead_time_days
fields.description: production_days + shipping_days

fields.name: is_selected
fields.description: True for the option chosen in the summary
